MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-15-2023 Continued To Nov-09-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Katherine Bearman, Esq., DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415-554-4256, kate.bearman@sfcityatty.org for Defendant CCSF,
Maria Yancich, Esq., JEANETTE N. LITTLE & ASSOCIATES, 925-225-6838, for Cross Defendant Francisco Romero Guerra

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT FRANCISCO GUERRA's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Cross-defendant Francisco Romero Guerra's "motion for determination of good faith settlement" is denied.

Key information was not provided in the moving papers. First, there was no "rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery" or "the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Second, while a policy-limits tender was disclosed, there was only sketchy information about the settlor's other "financial conditions." (Id.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion Opposing Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-15-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference

Maria Yancich, Esq., JEANETTE N. LITTLE & ASSOCIATES, 925-225-6838, for Cross Defendant Francisco Romero Guerra

Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Notice Of Motion Opposing Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Off calendar. The motion was not timely filed and served per CCP 1005.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

No appearance.

Defendant's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. This will serve interests of justice and efficiency. No reason exists to delay the November 6, 2023 trial date.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)
